title.en: Medical Latin & Greek — Core Lexicon and Clinical Terms
title.de: Medical Latin & Greek — Grundwortschatz und klinische Termini

description.en: Foundational core deck for first-year Medical Latin and Greek. Covers essential anatomical and clinical vocabulary, basic dictionary-form recognition, high-frequency Greek and Latin roots, and the interpretation of common medical compound terms. Designed to establish the lexical base required for later work in declensions, adjective agreement, stem formation, and exam-oriented terminology analysis.
description.de: Grundlegendes Kerndeck für Medizinisches Latein und Griechisch im ersten Studienjahr. Es vermittelt zentrale anatomische und klinische Terminologie, die Erkennung grundlegender Wörterbuchformen, häufige griechische und lateinische Wortstämme sowie die Deutung gebräuchlicher medizinischer Komposita. Das Deck schafft die lexikalische Grundlage für die spätere Arbeit mit Deklinationen, Adjektivkongruenz, Stammbildung und prüfungsorientierter Terminologieanalyse.

question.en: What is the English meaning of 'cor'?

explanation.en: cor = heart. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: cor, cordis n. Not a compound. Example: cor adiposum = fatty heart.

question.en: What is the dictionary entry of 'ren'?

explanation.en: ren = kidney. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: ren, renis m. Not a compound. Example: arteria renalis = renal artery.
pulmo = lung. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: pulmo, pulmonis m. Not a compound. Example: exsectio pulmonis = excision of the lung.

question.en: What is the meaning of 'os, ossis'?

explanation.en: os, ossis = bone. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: os, ossis n. Not a compound. Example: substantia ossea = bony substance.

question.en: What is the meaning of 'os, oris'?

explanation.en: os, oris = mouth. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: os, oris n. Not a compound. High-yield contrast: os, ossis = bone. Example: atresia oris = congenital absence/closure of the mouth.
caput = head. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: caput, capitis n. Not a compound. Example: caput femoris = head of femur.
sanguis = blood. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: sanguis, sanguinis m. Not a compound. Example: hyperlipidaemia = excess of fat in the blood.

question.en: What is the English meaning of 'cutis'?

explanation.en: cutis = skin. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension feminine: cutis, is f. Not a compound. Example: laesio cutis = lesion of the skin.
musculus = muscle. Simple Latin noun, 2nd declension masculine: musculus, i m. Not a compound. Example: musculus flexor pollicis longus.
nervus = nerve. Simple Latin noun, 2nd declension masculine: nervus, i m. Not a compound. Example: radiculitis = inflammation of a nerve root.
cortex = outer layer of an organ or structure. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: cortex, corticis m. Example: cortex cerebri = cerebral cortex.
thorax = chest. Greek noun used in medical terminology, 3rd declension masculine in Latinized form: thorax, acis m. Example: musculi thoracis = muscles of the chest.
adeps = fat, lard. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: adeps, adipis m. Example: inflammatio adipis = inflammation of adipose tissue.

question.en: What is the Greek-derived term for 'nail' in the worksheet?

explanation.en: onyx = nail (Greek). 3rd declension masculine in Latinized medical use: onyx, onychis m. Example: pachyonychia = abnormal thickening of the nails.
articulatio = joint. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension feminine: articulatio, onis f. Example: polyarthritis = inflammation of many joints.
pruritus = itching, itch. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension masculine: pruritus, us m. Example: pruritus cutis = itching of the skin.
luxatio = dislocation. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension feminine: luxatio, onis f. Example: luxatio lentis = dislocation of the lens.
collum = neck. Simple Latin noun, 2nd declension neuter: collum, i n. Example: collum femoris = neck of femur.

question.en: What is the Latin noun for 'spleen'?

explanation.en: lien = spleen. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: lien, lienis m. Example: splenitis / lienitis = inflammation of the spleen.
foramen = opening, aperture, perforation. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: foramen, inis n. Example: foramen caecum linguae.
semen = seminal fluid, sperm. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: semen, inis n. Example: semen virile.

question.en: What is the dictionary entry of 'pus'?

explanation.en: pus = pus. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: pus, puris n. Example: pyosalpinx = pus in the fallopian tube; pyorrhoea = discharge of pus.
crus = leg, crus. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: crus, cruris n. Example: crus sinistrum diaphragmatis = left crus of diaphragm.
ulcus = ulcer, lesion of skin or mucous membrane. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: ulcus, eris n. Example: ulcus ventriculi.
lac = milk. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: lac, lactis n. Example adjectives: lacteus, lactifer, lactivus.

cards.correctAnswer: collection of fluid in a body cavity
options: collection of fluid in a body cavity

explanation.en: hydrops = collection of fluid in a body cavity. Greek-derived noun in Latin medical usage, 3rd declension masculine: hydrops, opis m. Example: hydrops fetalis.
febris = fever. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension feminine: febris, is f. Example: antifebrilis = antipyretic / antifever.
pelvis = basin-like or cup-shaped cavity; also pelvis. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension feminine: pelvis, is f. Example: pyel- / pyelo- refers to renal pelvis.
ignis = fire. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: ignis, is m. Important as a model pure i-stem.

question.en: What is the Greek noun for 'vomiting'?

explanation.en: emesis = vomiting. Greek-derived noun used in medical Latin, 3rd declension feminine: emesis, is f. Example: hyperemesis gravidarum.

options: presence of excess fat in the blood
deficiency of all blood cell components

explanation.en: haematemesis = vomiting of blood. Greek compound: haemat- (blood) + emesis (vomiting). 3rd declension feminine in Latinized use: haematemesis, is f.
anamnesis = medical history of a disease or illness. Greek-derived noun, 3rd declension feminine: anamnesis, is f. Not a simple anatomical noun, but exam-relevant terminology.
mycosis = disease caused by a fungus. Greek compound: myc- (fungus) + -osis (abnormal condition). 3rd declension feminine in Latinized form: mycosis, is f.
sclerosis = induration, hardening. Greek compound: scler- + -osis. 3rd declension feminine in Latinized form: sclerosis, is f.
necrosis = death of cells, tissues, or organs. Greek compound: necr- + -osis. 3rd declension feminine in Latinized form: necrosis, is f.
lithiasis = formation of calculi of any kind. Greek compound: lith- (stone) + -iasis (abnormal condition). 3rd declension feminine in Latinized form: lithiasis, is f.
calcar = spur-like process. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: calcar, aris n. Example: calcar avis.
unguis = nail (Latin). Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: unguis, is m. Important contrast with Greek onyx, onychis m.
testis = one of the two male reproductive glands. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension masculine: testis, is m. Example: ptosis testis = downward displacement of the testes.
fel = gall, bile. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension neuter: fel, fellis n. Example: choledocholithiasis = gallstones in the common bile duct.
pestis = plague, pestilence. Simple Latin noun, 3rd declension feminine: pestis, is f.

cards.correctAnswer: decay and disintegration of a tooth
options: decay and disintegration of a tooth

explanation.en: caries = decay and disintegration of a tooth. Simple Latin noun, 5th declension feminine: caries, ei f.

options: decay and disintegration of a tooth
softening of an organ or part

explanation.en: canities = grayness or whiteness of hair. Simple Latin noun, 5th declension feminine: canities, ei f.

cards.correctAnswer: softness of an organ or part
options: softness of an organ or part
hardening of an organ or part
narrowing of a duct or aperture

explanation.en: mollities = softness of any organ or part. Simple Latin noun, 5th declension feminine: mollities, ei f.

cards.correctAnswer: infectious fatal viral disease carried by animals
options: infectious fatal viral disease carried by animals

explanation.en: rabies = rabies. Simple Latin noun, 5th declension feminine: rabies, ei f.

cards.correctAnswer: contagious skin disease with itching and papules
options: contagious skin disease with itching and papules
fatal viral disease transmitted by animals

explanation.en: scabies = contagious skin disease marked by itching, inflammation, and papules. Simple Latin noun, 5th declension feminine: scabies, ei f.
arcus = arch, arc. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension masculine: arcus, us m. Example: arcus longitudinalis pedis.
plexus = network of nerves or vessels. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension masculine: plexus, us m. Example: plexus cardiacus profundus.
processus = projection, outgrowth, process. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension masculine: processus, us m.

question.en: What is the dictionary entry of 'manus'?

explanation.en: manus = hand. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension feminine: manus, us f. Example: manus manum lavat.
visus = the act of seeing, sight. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension masculine: visus, us m.
auditus = hearing. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension masculine: auditus, us m. Example: hypoacusia = impaired hearing.

question.en: What does 'casus' mean in medical terminology?

explanation.en: casus = falling. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension masculine: casus, us m. Relevant for semantic contrast with Greek -ptosis.
olfactus = sense of smell. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension masculine: olfactus, us m. Example: hyperosmia = unusually acute smell.
genu = knee. Simple Latin noun, 4th declension neuter: genu, us n. Example: gonarthritis = inflammation of the knee joint.
facies = face, surface, appearance. Simple Latin noun, 5th declension feminine: facies, ei f. Example: facies cerebralis ossis temporalis.
species = appearance, kind. Simple Latin noun, 5th declension feminine: species, ei f.
series = row, series. Simple Latin noun, 5th declension feminine: series, ei f.
erythrocytus = red blood cell. Compound term of Greek origin: erythr- (red) + cyt- (cell), Latinized as a 2nd declension masculine noun: erythrocytus, i m.
mesocardia = location of the heart in the middle of the thorax. Greek compound: meso- (middle) + -cardia (heart condition/location). 1st declension noun in Latinized form.

options: atrophy and softening of the nails

explanation.en: pachyonychia = thick nails. Greek compound: pachy- (thick) + onych- (nail) + -ia. 1st declension noun in Latinized use.

cards.correctAnswer: disease of unknown cause / idiopathic disease
options: disease of unknown cause / idiopathic disease

explanation.en: idiopathia = idiopathic disease. Greek compound: idio- (one's own, peculiar) + -pathia (disease/suffering). 1st declension noun in Latinized form.

options: study of old age and elderly

explanation.en: neonatologia = study of newborns. Hybrid compound: neo- (new, Greek) + natus (born, Latin) + -logia (study, Greek). 1st declension noun in Latinized form.

cards.correctAnswer: inflammation of gray matter, brain, meninges, and spinal cord
options: inflammation of gray matter, brain, meninges, and spinal cord
inflammation of nerve roots, meninges, and spinal cord
inflammation of the brain and spinal cord
inflammation of the meninges and spinal cord

explanation.en: polioencephalomeningomyelitis is a Greek compound: polio- (gray matter) + encephalo- (brain) + meningo- (meninges) + myel- (spinal cord) + -itis (inflammation). High-yield long compound term.

cards.correctAnswer: condition of having a wide jaw
options: condition of having a wide jaw
condition of having a narrow jaw
condition of having a short nose
condition of having a protruding chin

explanation.en: platygnathus = broad jaw. Greek compound, Latinized as a 2nd declension masculine noun: platy- (broad) + gnath- (jaw) + -us.

cards.correctAnswer: inflammation of nerve roots, meninges, and spinal cord
options: inflammation of nerve roots, meninges, and spinal cord
inflammation of gray matter, brain, and meninges
inflammation of the brain and spinal cord only
inflammation of spinal cord and nerve roots

explanation.en: radiculomeningomyelitis = inflammation of nerve root(s), meninges, and spinal cord. Hybrid compound: radicul- (Latin radicula) + meningo- + myel- + -itis.

cards.correctAnswer: surgical fixation / suture of the cervix uteri
options: surgical fixation / suture of the cervix uteri
surgical suture of the uterine body
surgical repair of the vaginal wall
surgical incision of the cervix uteri

explanation.en: trachelorrhaphia = surgical repair or suturing of a lacerated cervix uteri (often post-childbirth). Greek compound: trachelo- (cervix/neck) + -rrhaphia (suture/seam). A 1st declension feminine noun in its Latinized form.

cards.correctAnswer: treatment / specialty concerned with the feet
options: treatment / specialty concerned with the feet
treatment / specialty concerned with the skin
treatment / specialty concerned with the eyes
treatment / specialty concerned with the nose

explanation.en: podiatria = treatment/healing of the feet. Greek compound: pod- (foot) + -iatria (healing/treatment). 1st declension noun in Latinized form.

options: study of old age and old people

explanation.en: anthropologia = anthropology. Greek compound: anthropo- (human being) + -logia (study). 1st declension noun in Latinized form.

cards.correctAnswer: presence of excess fat in the blood
options: presence of excess fat in the blood
presence of excess glucose in the blood
presence of excess uric acid in the blood
deficiency of plasma proteins in the blood

explanation.en: lipaemia = fat in the blood; hyperlipaemia = excess of fats/lipids in blood. Greek compound: lip- (fat) + -aemia (blood condition). 1st declension noun in Latinized use.
cardiomyopathia = disease/suffering of heart muscle. Greek compound: cardio- + myo- + -pathia. Latin synonym noted in the worksheet: myocardiopathia.

options: inflammation of the chest wall fascia

explanation.en: thoracomyositis = inflammation of the chest muscles. Greek compound: thoraco- (chest) + myos- (muscle) + -itis.

question.en: What is 'atresia oris' conceptually referring to?

cards.correctAnswer: congenital absence / closure of the mouth
options: congenital absence / closure of the mouth
congenital absence / closure of the esophagus
congenital absence / closure of the anus
congenital absence / closure of the nasal passages

explanation.en: atresia oris = condition of congenital absence/closure of the mouth. Hybrid idea: Greek atresia + Latin os, oris.

cards.correctAnswer: plastic repair of the lips and mouth
options: plastic repair of the lips and mouth
plastic repair of the nose and palate
plastic repair of the ear and jaw
plastic repair of the eyelids and orbit

explanation.en: cheilostomatoplastica = plastic surgery/restoration of the lips and mouth. Greek compound: cheilo- (lip) + stomato- (mouth) + -plastica.

options: accumulation of pus in a cavity

explanation.en: pyorrhoea = discharge of pus. Greek compound: pyo- (pus) + -rrhoea (flow/discharge). 1st declension noun in Latinized form.
pyosalpinx = pus in the fallopian (uterine) tube. Greek compound: pyo- + salpinx/salping-.

cards.correctAnswer: incision into the gallbladder through the abdominal wall
options: incision into the gallbladder through the abdominal wall
removal of the gallbladder through the abdominal wall
suture of the gallbladder through the abdominal wall
drainage of the gallbladder through the abdominal wall

explanation.en: laparocholecystotomia = incision into the gallbladder through the abdominal wall. Greek compound: laparo- (abdomen) + cholecysto- (gallbladder) + -tomia (incision).

cards.correctAnswer: condition in which a human imagines being an animal
options: condition in which a human imagines being an animal
abnormal dislike or hatred of children

explanation.en: zoanthropia = delusion of being an animal. Greek compound: zoo- (animal) + anthrop- (human) + -ia.

cards.correctAnswer: inflammation of the renal pelvis and kidney
options: inflammation of the renal pelvis and kidney

explanation.en: pyelonephritis = inflammation of the renal pelvis and kidney. Greek compound: pyelo- (renal pelvis) + nephr- (kidney) + -itis.

cards.correctAnswer: treatment by artificial raising of body temperature
options: treatment by artificial raising of body temperature
treatment by artificial lowering of body temperature
treatment by controlled maintenance of normal temperature

explanation.en: pyretotherapia = heat/fever treatment. Greek compound: pyreto- (fever/heat) + -therapia. A synonym family includes hyperthermia-based therapy terms.
antipyreticus = antipyretic. Greek compound with adjective ending of 1st/2nd declension: anti- (against) + pyret- (fever) + -icus, a, um.
antitussicus = antitussive. Hybrid compound possible in the worksheet: anti- (Greek) + tuss- (Latin stem of tussis, cough) + -icus.

options: abnormal dislike or hatred of children
condition in which a human imagines being an animal

explanation.en: thalassophobia = fear of the sea. Greek compound: thalasso- (sea) + -phobia (fear).
pyelectasia = dilation of renal pelvis. Greek compound: pyel- + -ectasia (dilation). A synonymous form may be built with another noun pattern in the workbook.

options: calcified gallstone in the biliary system

explanation.en: lithopaedion = stone baby. Greek compound, neuter noun of 2nd declension in Latinized form: litho- (stone) + paedion (little child/infant).

cards.correctAnswer: abnormal dislike or hatred of children
options: abnormal dislike or hatred of children

explanation.en: misopaedia = hatred of children. Greek compound: miso- (hatred) + paed- (child) + -ia.

cards.correctAnswer: condition of having only one set of teeth
options: condition of having only one set of teeth
condition of having two sets of teeth
condition of having extra supernumerary teeth
condition of complete absence of teeth

explanation.en: monophyodontia = having only one dentition. Greek compound: mono- (one) + phy- (grow) + odont- (tooth) + -ia.

cards.correctAnswer: condition of having two sets of teeth
options: condition of having two sets of teeth
condition of having only one set of teeth
condition of having extra supernumerary teeth

explanation.en: diphyodontia = having two sets of teeth (primary and permanent). Greek compound: di- (two) + phy- + odont- + -ia.
cholecystorrhaphia = suture of the gallbladder. Greek compound: cholecysto- + -rrhaphia.

cards.correctAnswer: downward displacement / abnormal position of testes
options: downward displacement / abnormal position of testes
congenital absence of one or both testes

explanation.en: cryptorchismus in broader terminology concerns hidden/undescended testis; in the worksheet the target notion is abnormal position/displacement of testes. Greek compound form with -ismus.
onychomycosis = fungal condition of the nails. Greek compound: onycho- (nail) + myc- (fungus) + -osis.

options: condition of imagining oneself an animal
condition of abnormal fear of open spaces

explanation.en: schizophrenia = split mind. Greek compound: schizo- (split) + phren- (mind) + -ia. High-yield classic medical Greek.
aphakia = absence of the crystalline lens of the eye. Greek compound: a- (without) + phak-/phac- (lens) + -ia.
polyarthritis = inflammation of all/many joints. Greek compound: poly- (many) + arthr- (joint) + -itis.

cards.correctAnswer: deficiency of all blood cell components
options: deficiency of all blood cell components
deficiency of red blood cells only
deficiency of white blood cells only

explanation.en: pancytopenia = deficiency of all three cellular blood components. Greek compound: pan- (all) + cyto- (cell) + -penia (poverty/deficiency).
tachyarrhythmia = fast irregular rhythm of the heart. Greek compound: tachy- (fast) + arrhythmia (loss/abnormality of rhythm).

options: abnormal narrowing of the nasal passages
abnormal deviation of the nasal septum

explanation.en: brachyrhinia = short nose. Greek compound: brachy- (short) + rhin- (nose) + -ia.

cards.correctAnswer: study of old age and old people
options: study of old age and old people

explanation.en: gerontologia = gerontology. Greek compound: geronto- (old age/old person) + -logia (study).

cards.correctAnswer: formation of tissue similar to that already existing
options: formation of tissue similar to that already existing
formation of tissue unlike that already existing
replacement of one tissue type by another

explanation.en: homoeoplasia = formation of the same/similar tissue. Greek compound: homeo-/homo- (same, alike) + -plasia (formation/growth).

options: complete absence of sense of smell
diminished or impaired sense of smell
distorted or perverted sense of smell

explanation.en: hyperosmia = unusually acute smell. Greek compound: hyper- (excessive) + -osmia (smell). High-yield sensory terminology from the worksheet.
